cited_sources.title: Interactions between bupropion and 3,4-Methylenedioxymethamphetamine in healthy subjects
cited_sources.authors: Schmid Y, Hysek CM, Simmler LD, Crockett MG, Quednow BB, Liechti ME

claims.claim: It demonstrates that **bupropion increases plasma concentrations of MDMA while simultaneously enhancing subjective mood effects and reducing certain cardiovascular responses** [oai_citation:0‡schmid-et-al-2015-Interactions between bupropion and 3, 4-methylenedioxymethamphetamine.pdf](sediment://file_00000000c9f4720a949bb562a10e825f) The findings provide strong evidence that: - **norepinephrine (NE)** mediates MDMA’s cardiostimulant effects - **serotonin (5-HT)** (not dopamine) primarily mediates subjective effects - **dopamine (DA) plays a minimal role in MDMA’s acute subjective effects** Additionally, the study identifies **bidirectional pharmacokinetic interactions**, with both drugs increasing each other’s plasma exposure.
claims.notes: Extracted from schmid_2015_bupropion_mdma_interactions using rule-based keyword matching.

cited_sources.title: Interactions between bupropion and 3,4-Methylenedioxymethamphetamine in healthy subjects
cited_sources.authors: Schmid Y, Hysek CM, Simmler LD, Crockett MG, Quednow BB, Liechti ME
cited_sources.citation_text: Schmid Y, Hysek CM, Simmler LD, Crockett MG, Quednow BB, Liechti ME. Interactions between bupropion and 3,4-Methylenedioxymethamphetamine in healthy subjects. Journal of Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics. 2015. doi:10.1124/jpet.114.222356.

claims.claim: --- ## Key Claims ### Claim 1 - claim: Bupropion increases plasma concentrations and prolongs the effects of MDMA via pharmacokinetic interaction - type: interaction - entities: [bupropion, MDMA] - directionality: increases MDMA exposure - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 2 - claim: MDMA increases plasma concentrations of bupropion and its metabolites - type: interaction - entities: [MDMA, bupropion] - directionality: bidirectional increase in exposure - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 3 - claim: Bupropion enhances subjective mood effects of MDMA despite dopamine transporter inhibition - type: mechanism - entities: [bupropion, MDMA, dopamine] - directionality: increases subjective effects - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 4 - claim: Dopamine is not a primary mediator of the acute subjective effects of MDMA in humans - type: mechanism - entities: [dopamine, MDMA] - directionality: minimal contribution - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 5 - claim: Norepinephrine mediates the cardiostimulant effects of MDMA - type: mechanism - entities: [norepinephrine, MDMA] - directionality: drives cardiovascular stimulation - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 6 - claim: Bupropion reduces MDMA-induced increases in heart rate and norepinephrine levels - type: interaction - entities: [bupropion, MDMA, norepinephrine] - directionality: decreases cardiovascular stimulation - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 7 - claim: Bupropion inhibits CYP2D6, leading to reduced MDMA metabolism and increased MDMA plasma levels - type: mechanism - entities: [bupropion, CYP2D6, MDMA] - directionality: decreases metabolism → increases MDMA levels - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ### Claim 8 - claim: Bupropion reduces formation of MDMA metabolites (HMMA, MDA) via enzyme inhibition - type: mechanism - entities: [bupropion, MDMA metabolites] - directionality: decreases metabolite formation - confidence: high - evidence_strength: strong --- ## Mechanisms Described - Dopamine transporter (DAT) inhibition by bupropion - Norepinephrine transporter (NET) inhibition - MDMA-induced monoamine release (5-HT, NE, DA) - CYP2D6 inhibition → reduced MDMA metabolism - CYP2B6 competitive interactions affecting metabolite formation - Bidirectional pharmacokinetic interaction between drugs --- ## Clinical / Practical Guidance - Co-administration of bupropion and MDMA: - increases MDMA exposure and duration - enhances subjective effects - reduces heart rate response - Dopaminergic blockade does **not** attenuate MDMA’s subjective effects - Cardiovascular risk may be partially reduced but **not eliminated** - Pharmacokinetic interactions may increase overall drug burden --- ## Contraindications / Risks - Increased MDMA plasma levels → higher toxicity risk - Increased bupropion exposure → potential accumulation effects - Prolonged psychoactive effects → extended physiological stress - Complex metabolic interactions via CYP enzymes - Potential unpredictability due to bidirectional PK interaction --- ## Interaction Relevance ### High-confidence interaction - bupropion + MDMA - PK: ↑ MDMA levels (CYP2D6 inhibition) - PK: ↑ bupropion levels (reciprocal effect) - PD: ↑ subjective effects - PD: ↓ cardiovascular stimulation --- ## Key Quantitative Findings - MDMA exposure: - Cmax ↑ ~15% - AUC ↑ ~30% - half-life prolonged - MDMA metabolites: - HMMA ↓ ~66–75% - MDA ↓ significantly - Bupropion exposure: - Cmax ↑ ~18% - AUC ↑ ~27% - Cardiovascular effects: - heart rate increase significantly reduced (see pharmacokinetic tables and figures, pp.

claims.claim: 4–7) [oai_citation:1‡schmid-et-al-2015-Interactions between bupropion and 3, 4-methylenedioxymethamphetamine.pdf](sediment://file_00000000c9f4720a949bb562a10e825f) --- ## Limitations - Small sample size (n = 16) - Single-dose MDMA condition - Moderate dopamine transporter occupancy (~26%) - Non-stereoselective pharmacokinetic analysis - Healthy subjects only (limited generalizability) --- ## Notes This is a **high-quality human clinical interaction study** and should be treated as: - Strong evidence for **PK + PD interaction modeling** - Key source for **MDMA interaction mechanisms** - Foundational for **dopamine vs serotonin role differentiation** Particularly important for: - validating CYP-mediated interaction rules - distinguishing NE vs DA contributions - modeling bidirectional drug interactions --- ## Extraction Notes - Strong PK interaction (bidirectional) - Strong PD dissociation: - ↓ cardiovascular effects - ↑ subjective effects - Confirms: - serotonin + norepinephrine dominance - dopamine non-essential for MDMA subjective effects
